text: In software development, testing plays a vital role in ensuring quality. Unit tests check small pieces of code individually, while integration tests verify that different parts of a system work together smoothly. One popular approach is test-driven development (TDD), where you write tests before writing the actual code. This process forces you to think carefully about what each function should do. All these practices give developers confidence. When changes are made to a project, a well-tested codebase helps catch errors early. Instead of worrying about breaking something, developers can make improvements more freely, knowing that the tests will alert them to any problems.
